title: Schliff v7.0 Week 1 — Multi-Format Support + --url Flag
goal: Enable schliff to score SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, AGENTS.md, and generic .md files with format-aware scoring. Add --url flag for remote file scoring.
architecture: FormatProfile frozen dataclass with function pointers for metadata extraction. Format registry with filename-based detection. Per-format composite weights. Zero new dependencies. Backward compatible — SKILL.md scores identically to v6.3.0.
tech_stack: Python 3.9+ stdlib only (dataclasses, re, pathlib, urllib.request)

create: skills/schliff/scripts/scoring/formats.py — FormatProfile dataclass, registry, detect_format(), metadata extractors (~150 LOC)
skills/schliff/tests/unit/test_formats.py — Format detection, metadata extraction, profile validation (~200 LOC)
modify: skills/schliff/scripts/shared.py:139-166 — build_scores() accepts optional fmt parameter
skills/schliff/scripts/shared.py:77-88 — extract_description() delegates to fmt if provided
skills/schliff/scripts/scoring/structure.py — score_structure() accepts fmt, uses format-specific checks
skills/schliff/scripts/cli.py — auto-detection, --format flag, --url flag, help text update
unchanged: scoring/patterns.py — regex library, no format awareness needed

steps.action: Write failing test for FormatProfile dataclass and SKILL.md detection
steps.code:
import pytest
from scoring.formats import FormatProfile, detect_format, FORMATS


def test_format_profile_is_frozen():
    fmt = FORMATS["skill.md"]
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        fmt.format_id = "hacked"


def test_detect_skill_md():
    fmt = detect_format("path/to/SKILL.md")
    assert fmt.format_id == "skill.md"
    assert fmt.has_frontmatter is True


def test_detect_skill_md_nested():
    fmt = detect_format("/home/user/.claude/skills/deploy/SKILL.md")
    assert fmt.format_id == "skill.md"


def test_skill_md_extract_name():
    content = '---\nname: my-skill\ndescription: A test skill\n---\n# Body'
    fmt = FORMATS["skill.md"]
    assert fmt.extract_name(content) == "my-skill"


def test_skill_md_extract_description():
    content = '---\nname: my-skill\ndescription: A test skill\n---\n# Body'
    fmt = FORMATS["skill.md"]
    assert fmt.extract_description(content) == "A test skill"


def test_skill_md_strip_metadata():
    content = '---\nname: my-skill\ndescription: A test\n---\n# Body here'
    fmt = FORMATS["skill.md"]
    body = fmt.strip_metadata(content)
    assert body.startswith("# Body here")
    assert "---" not in body

steps.code_to_append:
# --- CLAUDE.md format ---

def test_detect_claude_md():
    fmt = detect_format("CLAUDE.md")
    assert fmt.format_id == "claude.md"
    assert fmt.has_frontmatter is False


def test_claude_md_extract_name():
    content = "# My Project Config\n\nAlways use TypeScript."
    fmt = FORMATS["claude.md"]
    assert fmt.extract_name(content) == "My Project Config"


def test_claude_md_extract_description():
    content = "# My Project\n\nThis project uses React and TypeScript."
    fmt = FORMATS["claude.md"]
    assert fmt.extract_description(content) == "This project uses React and TypeScript."


def test_claude_md_strip_metadata_is_identity():
    content = "# Title\n\nBody text here."
    fmt = FORMATS["claude.md"]
    assert fmt.strip_metadata(content) == content


# --- .cursorrules format ---

def test_detect_cursorrules():
    fmt = detect_format(".cursorrules")
    assert fmt.format_id == "cursorrules"
    assert fmt.has_frontmatter is False


def test_cursorrules_extract_description():
    content = "You are a senior Python developer. Always use type hints."
    fmt = FORMATS["cursorrules"]
    assert "senior Python developer" in fmt.extract_description(content)


# --- AGENTS.md format ---

def test_detect_agents_md():
    fmt = detect_format("AGENTS.md")
    assert fmt.format_id == "agents.md"
    assert fmt.has_frontmatter is True


def test_agents_md_with_frontmatter():
    content = '---\nname: deploy-agent\ndescription: Deploys to prod\n---\n# Deploy Agent'
    fmt = FORMATS["agents.md"]
    assert fmt.extract_name(content) == "deploy-agent"
    assert fmt.extract_description(content) == "Deploys to prod"


def test_agents_md_without_frontmatter():
    content = "# Deploy Agent\n\nHandles production deployments."
    fmt = FORMATS["agents.md"]
    assert fmt.extract_name(content) == "Deploy Agent"
    assert fmt.extract_description(content) == "Handles production deployments."


# --- Generic fallback ---

def test_detect_generic_md():
    fmt = detect_format("README.md")
    assert fmt.format_id == "generic"


def test_detect_generic_unknown():
    fmt = detect_format("random-file.txt")
    assert fmt.format_id == "generic"


def test_all_formats_have_composite_weights():
    for fmt_id, fmt in FORMATS.items():
        total = sum(fmt.composite_weights.values())
        assert 0.95 <= total <= 1.05, f"{fmt_id} weights sum to {total}"

tasks.name: Wire FormatProfile into build_scores() and composite

steps.action: Write regression test: SKILL.md scores must be identical to v6.3.0
steps.code_to_append:
def test_skill_md_scores_identical_to_v6(tmp_path):
    """Regression: SKILL.md format must produce identical scores to v6.3.0."""
    import sys
    scripts_dir = str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "scripts")
    if scripts_dir not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, scripts_dir)
    from shared import build_scores
    from scoring.composite import compute_composite

    skill = tmp_path / "SKILL.md"
    skill.write_text('---\nname: test\ndescription: A test skill\n---\n# Test\n\n## Rules\n\n1. Do X because Y.\n')

    # Score without fmt (v6 behavior)
    scores_v6 = build_scores(str(skill))
    composite_v6 = compute_composite(scores_v6)

    # Score with explicit fmt (v7 behavior)
    from scoring.formats import FORMATS
    scores_v7 = build_scores(str(skill), fmt=FORMATS["skill.md"])
    composite_v7 = compute_composite(scores_v7, format_weights=FORMATS["skill.md"].composite_weights)

    assert composite_v6["score"] == composite_v7["score"], (
        f"v6={composite_v6['score']} != v7={composite_v7['score']}")

steps.action: Modify build_scores() to accept optional fmt parameter
steps.change: Line 139: Add fmt parameter. When fmt is provided, pass fmt.extract_description to triggers scorer. When fmt is None, use existing behavior.
steps.code_diff:
Replace lines 139-166:

def build_scores(skill_path: str, eval_suite: Optional[dict] = None,
                  include_runtime: bool = False, fmt=None) -> dict:
    from scoring import (
        score_structure, score_triggers, score_efficiency,
        score_composability, score_quality, score_edges,
        score_clarity,
    )

    scores = {
        "structure": score_structure(skill_path, fmt=fmt),
        "triggers": score_triggers(skill_path, eval_suite, fmt=fmt),
        "quality": score_quality(skill_path, eval_suite),
        "edges": score_edges(skill_path, eval_suite),
        "efficiency": score_efficiency(skill_path),
        "composability": score_composability(skill_path),
        "clarity": score_clarity(skill_path),
    }

    if include_runtime:
        from scoring import score_runtime
        scores["runtime"] = score_runtime(skill_path, eval_suite, enabled=False)

    return scores

steps.action: Write tests for format-specific structure scoring
test_cases: CLAUDE.md with no frontmatter should NOT lose frontmatter points
.cursorrules (plain text) should score on efficiency/imperative only
AGENTS.md with optional frontmatter should handle both cases
steps.note: The structure scorer currently gives 30 of 100 points for frontmatter. For formats without frontmatter (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules), these points must be redistributed to other checks.

steps.change: When fmt is provided and has_frontmatter is False, skip frontmatter checks and redistribute those 30 points to other checks (length, examples, headers, imperative_voice). When fmt is None, use existing SKILL.md behavior.
